review: The angled jar wrench with the silicone grip is a game-changer for mornings when my RA hands are stiff. Opens tight jars without triggering wrist pain. Shipping was fast; local Murfreesboro pickup would've been faster but USPS worked fine.

The angled vegetable peeler took some of the strain off my wrist while prepping sweet potatoes and broccoli. Wide handle really matters. I'm a nurse with rheumatoid arthritis and this is the kind of small tool that keeps me cooking after a long clinic day.

The five-piece grip-aid set covers everything I struggle with on stiff mornings: can lids, jar tops, water bottles, pull tabs, and pop-top rings. Well worth the $41.60. Highly recommend for anyone managing RA in the kitchen.
The wide-grip silicone utensil set has been in my utensil crock for a year now. The chunky handles are exactly what I needed for arthritis-friendly cooking. Spatula and tongs get the most use.
